should_trigger: Design an auto-orchestrator where a planner, three tool workers, and a reviewer coordinate without sharing mutable state. Specify the message contracts and deadlock checks.
Our agents sometimes block forever because the worker sends a result before the coordinator is ready, while the coordinator is waiting for a different event. Use CSP to repair the protocol.
Model tool rate limits as a resource manager process that accepts guarded requests from many workers and grants access only when capacity is available.
We need backpressure between a document producer and summarizer so the producer cannot outrun the consumer. Design the rendezvous or bounded-buffer process.
A coordinator must choose among user cancellation, tool completion, timeout, and reviewer approval events. Define guarded choices and starvation rules.
should_not_trigger: Create a business KPI dashboard for workflow completion times and make the charts easier to scan.
Design a database transaction boundary for state update plus event publication after an agent finishes work.
Summarize C. A. R. Hoare's career and the historical impact of CSP in one paragraph.
edge_cases: The team wants to use a message queue between every agent. Decide which interactions need buffering and which need synchronous backpressure or explicit acknowledgement.
A workflow has only one worker and one tool call, but a cancellation event can arrive while the tool is running. Decide whether CSP modeling is useful and specify the minimal process protocol.
The orchestrator runtime says it schedules fairly. Evaluate whether the design can rely on that or needs explicit priority, aging, or round-robin selection rules.
